This catches beads orphaned by workers\n33\t/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n34\t/// cleaned up).\n35\t///\n36\t/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n37\t/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n38\t/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n39\t///\n40\t/// # Arguments\n41\t/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n42\t/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n43\t/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n44\t/// * `qualified_id` - This worker's fully-qualified identity (excluded from orphan detection)\n45\t///\n46\t/// # Returns\n47\t/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n48\t/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\n49\tpub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n50\t    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n51\t    registry: &Registry,\n52\t    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n53\t    qualified_id: &str,\n54\t) -> Result<u32> {\n55\t    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n56\t    let workers = registry.list()?;\n57\t\n58\t    // Build a set of fully-qualified worker IDs for registered, alive workers.\n59\t    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n60\t        .iter()\n61\t        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n62\t        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n63\t        .collect();\n64\t\n65\t    let mut released = 0u32;\n66\t\n67\t    for bead in &all_beads {\n68\t        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n69\t            continue;\n70\t        }\n71\t\n72\t        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n73\t            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n74\t            _ => continue,\n75\t        };\n76\t\n77\t        // Skip if the assignee matches our own qualified identity (we're running).\n78\t        if assignee == qualified_id {\n79\t            continue;\n80\t        }\n81\t\n82\t        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n83\t        // Workers register with fully-qualified IDs ({adapter}-{worker_id}),\n84\t        // so this comparison prevents collisions when workers from different\n85\t        // adapter pools share a NATO name.\n86\t        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n87\t            continue;\n88\t        }\n89\t\n90\t        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n91\t        tracing::info!(\n92\t            bead_id = %bead.id,\n93\t            assignee = %assignee,\n94\t            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n95\t            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n96\t        );\n97\t\n98\t        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n99\t            Ok(()) => {\n100\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n101\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n102\t                    reason: format!(\"orphaned: assignee {} has no live worker\", assignee),\n103\t                });\n104\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n105\t                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n106\t                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n107\t                });\n108\t                released += 1;\n109\t            }\n110\t            Err(e) => {\n111\t                tracing::warn!(\n112\t                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n113\t                    error = %e,\n114\t                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n115\t                );\n116\t                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendBeadReleaseFailed {\n117\t                    bead_id: bead.id.to_string(),\n118\t                    assignee: assignee.clone(),\n119\t                    error: e.to_string(),\n120\t                });\n121\t            }\n122\t        }\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    Ok(released)\n126\t}\n127\t\n128\t/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n129\t#[derive(Debug, Default)]\n130\tstruct MendSummary {\n131\t    beads_released: u32,\n132\t    locks_removed: u32,\n133\t    deps_cleaned: u32,\n134\t    db_repaired: bool,\n135\t    db_rebuilt: bool,\n136\t    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n137\t    traces_pruned: u32,\n138\t    traces_cleaned: u32,\n139\t    learnings_pruned: u32,\n140\t    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n141\t    orphaned_heartbeats_removed: u32,\n142\t    workers_deregistered: u32,\n143\t    idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n144\t    rate_limits_cleaned: u32,\n145\t    rate_limit_providers_removed: u32,\n146\t    rate_limit_providers_reset: u32,\n147\t}\n148\t\n149\timpl MendSummary {\n150\t    /// Whether mend performed work that changes bead store state.\n151\t    ///\n152\t    /// Only operations that add or release claimable beads should return true.\n153\t    /// A strand MUST return `WorkCreated` only when it inserts a claimable bead\n154\t    /// into the store that the waterfall will re-scan. No-op cleanup (pruning\n155\t    /// traces, removing stale locks, repairing DB) should return `NoWork`.\n156\t    ///\n157\t    /// Operations that return `WorkCreated`:\n158\t    /// - `beads_released > 0`: Orphaned beads were released back to Open status.\n159\t    /// - `deps_cleaned > 0`: Stale dependency links were removed (beads become claimable).\n160\t    ///\n161\t    /// Operations that return `NoWork` (maintenance, not work creation):\n162\t    /// - `locks_removed > 0`: Lock file cleanup (doesn't add beads to queue).\n163\t    /// - `db_repaired`: Doctor repair Err(e) => {\n321\t                tracing::debug!(\n322\t                    error = %e,\n323\t                    \"mend: failed to read heartbeat directory for orphaned heartbeat cleanup\"\n324\t                );\n325\t                return Ok(());\n326\t            }\n327\t        };\n328\t\n329\t        // Get all registered worker IDs.\n330\t        let registered_ids = match self.registry.list() {\n331\t            Ok(workers) => {\n332\t                let mut ids = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n333\t                for w in workers {\n334\t                    ids.insert(w.id);\n335\t                }\n336\t                ids\n337\t            }\n338\t            Err(e) => {\n339\t                tracing::warn!(\n340\t                    error = %e,\n341\t                    \"mend: failed to read worker registry for orphaned heartbeat cleanup\"\n342\t                );\n343\t                return Ok(());\n344\t            }\n345\t        };\n346\t\n347\t        for hb in heartbeats {\n348\t            // Use qualified_id for comparison; fall back to worker_id for old heartbeats.\n349\t            let worker_key = if hb.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n350\t                &hb.worker_id\n351\t            } else {\n352\t                &hb.qualified_id\n353\t            };\n354\t\n355\t            // Skip if this heartbeat (shouldn't happen, but be safe).\n361\t            if worker_key == &self.qualified_id {\n362\t                continue;\n363\t            }\n364\t\n365\t            // Check if the heartbeat is stale (older than TTL).\n366\t            // Only remove stale heartbeats to avoid deleting recently orphaned files.\n367\t            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(&hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n368\t                continue;\n369\t            }\n370\t\n371\t            // Heartbeat is orphaned and stale — remove it.\n372\t            let heartbeat_path = hb\n373\t                .heartbeat_file\n374\t                .clone()\n375\t                .unwrap_or_else(|| self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", worker_key)));\n376\t\n377\t            let age_secs = match std::fs::remove_file(&heartbeat_path) {\n383\t                Ok(()) => {\n384\t                    tracing::info!(\n385\t                        worker_id = %worker_key,\n386\t                        path = %heartbeat_path.display(),\n387\t                        age_secs,\n388\t                        \"removed orphaned heartbeat file\"\n389\t                    );\n390\t\n391\t    // ── Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal ───────────────────────────────────\n414\t\n415\t    /// Remove claim lock files that are not actively held by any process.\n416\t    ///\n417\t    /// This function immediately cleans up lock files whose holding process\n418\t    /// has died, without waiting for an age-based timeout. The flock(2) lock\n419\t    /// is automatically released by the kernel when the holder process dies,\n420\t    /// so try_acquire_flock() will succeed immediately and we can remove the\n421\t    /// stale metadata file.\n422\t    ///\n423\t    /// Lock files older than lock_ttl_secs are also cleaned up as a fallback\n424\t    /// to handle edge cases where the flock probe might fail.\n425\t    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n426\t        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n427\t\n428\t        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n429\t            Ok(e) => e,\n430\t            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n431\t            Err(e) => {\n432\t                tracing::warn!(\n433\t                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n434\t                    error = %e,\n435\t                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n436\t                );\n437\t                return Ok(());\n438\t            }\n439\t        };\n440\t\n441\t        for entry in entries {\n442\t            let entry = match entry {\n443\t                Ok(e) => e,\n444\t                Err(_) => continue,\n445\t            };\n446\t\n447\t            let path = entry.path();\n448\t            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n449\t                Some(n) => n,\n450\t                None => continue,\n451\t            };\n452\t\n453\t            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n454\t            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n455\t                continue;\n456\t            }\n457\t\n458\t            // Check file age for logging/fallback cleanup.\n459\t            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n460\t                Some(age) => age,\n461\t                None => continue,\n462\t            };\n463\t\n464\t            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n465\t            // no one is holding it — safe to delete immediately.\n466\t            //\n467\t            // The kernel releases flocks when the holder process dies, so this\n468\t            // probe succeeds immediately for dead PIDs regardless of file age.\n469\t            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n470\t                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n471\t                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n472\t                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n473\t                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n474\t                        tracing::warn!(\n475\t                            path = %path.display(),\n476\t                            error = %e,\n477\t                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n478\t                        );\n479\t                        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLockRemoveFailed {\n480\t                            lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n481\t                            error: e.to_string(),\n482\t                        });\n483\t                        continue;\n484\t                    }\n485\t\n486\t                    tracing::info!(\n487\t                        path = %path.display(),\n488\t                        age_secs,\n489\t                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n490\t                    );\n491\t\n492\t                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n493\t                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n494\t                        age_secs,\n495\t                    });\n496\t\n497\t                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n498\t                }\n499\t                Ok(None) => {\n500\t                    // Lock is actively held — skip."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"be91b0f0-a74a-4db6-99c2-a69ec602ba4d","uuid":"dcdeb2a8-5b59-4645-bce2-5313db1cc39a","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:35:33.990Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/strand/mend.rs","content":"//! Mend strand: maintenance and self-healing.\n//!\n//! Strand 2 in the waterfall. Runs after Pluck finds no work. Cleans up\n//! stale claims, orphaned locks, broken dependency links, and database\n//! corruption. If any cleanup is performed, returns `WorkCreated` so the\n//! waterfall restarts from Pluck (released beads may now be claimable).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `bead_store`, `config`, `health`, `peer`, `registry`,\n//!             `telemetry`, `types`, `trace`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\nuse std::time::{Duration, SystemTime};\n\nuse anyhow::Result;\nuse chrono::Utc;\n\nuse crate::bead_store::BeadStore;\nuse crate::config::{LimitsConfig, MendConfig};\nuse crate::health::HealthMonitor;\nuse crate::learning::LearningsFile;\nuse crate::peer::PeerMonitor;\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\nuse crate::telemetry::{EventKind, Telemetry};\nuse crate::trace::cleanup_traces;\nuse crate::types::{BeadStatus, StrandError, StrandResult};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Store-scoped orphan cleanup (shared between Mend and Explore)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Scan all in-progress beads in a store and release any whose assignee does\n/// not correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n/// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was already\n/// cleaned up).\n///\n/// This is a store-scoped function that can be called against any BeadStore,\n/// not just the home workspace. Used by both MendStrand (for home) and\n/// ExploreStrand (for remote workspaces).\n///\n/// # Arguments\n/// * `store` - The bead store to scan (can be home or remote)\n/// * `registry` - Worker registry for live worker lookup\n/// * `telemetry` - Telemetry emitter for orphan release events\n/// * `qualified_id` - This worker's fully-qualified identity (excluded from orphan detection)\n///\n/// # Returns\n/// * `Ok(u32)` - Number of orphans released\n/// * `Err(anyhow::Error)` - Store read failure\npub async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n    store: &dyn BeadStore,\n    registry: &Registry,\n    telemetry: &Telemetry,\n    qualified_id: &str,\n) -> Result<u32> {\n    let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n    let workers = registry.list()?;\n\n    // Build a set of fully-qualified worker IDs for registered, alive workers.\n    let live_worker_ids: std::collections::HashSet<String> = workers\n        .iter()\n        .filter(|w| HealthMonitor::check_pid_alive(w.pid))\n        .map(|w| w.id.clone())\n        .collect();\n\n    let mut released = 0u32;\n\n    for bead in &all_beads {\n        if bead.status != BeadStatus::InProgress {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        let assignee = match &bead.assignee {\n            Some(a) if !a.is_empty() => a,\n            _ => continue,\n        };\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches our own qualified identity (we're running).\n        if assignee == qualified_id {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Skip if the assignee matches a registered, alive worker.\n        // Workers register with fully-qualified IDs ({adapter}-{worker_id}),\n        // so this comparison prevents collisions when workers from different\n        // adapter pools share a NATO name.\n        if live_worker_ids.contains(assignee.as_str()) {\n            continue;\n        }\n\n        // Orphaned: assignee is not a live registered worker. Release it.\n        tracing::info!(\n            bead_id = %bead.id,\n            assignee = %assignee,\n            workspace = %bead.workspace.display(),\n            \"releasing orphaned in-progress bead (assignee has no live worker)\"\n        );\n\n        match store.release(&bead.id).await {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::BeadReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    reason: format!(\"orphaned: assignee {} has no live worker\", assignee),\n                });\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::StuckReleased {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                    peer_worker: assignee.clone(),\n                });\n                released += 1;\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    bead_id = %bead.id,\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to release orphaned in-progress bead\"\n                );\n                let _ = telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendBeadReleaseFailed {\n                    bead_id: bead.id.to_string(),\n                    assignee: assignee.clone(),\n                    error: e.to_string(),\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    Ok(released)\n}\n\n/// Summary of work performed during one Mend evaluation cycle.\n#[derive(Debug, Default)]\nstruct MendSummary {\n    beads_released: u32,\n    locks_removed: u32,\n    deps_cleaned: u32,\n    db_repaired: bool,\n    db_rebuilt: bool,\n    agent_logs_cleaned: u32,\n    traces_pruned: u32,\n    traces_cleaned: u32,\n    learnings_pruned: u32,\n    learnings_consolidated: u32,\n    orphaned_heartbeats_removed: u32,\n    workers_deregistered: u32,\n    idle_workers_flagged: u32,\n    rate_limits_cleaned: u32,\n    rate_limit_providers_removed: u32,\n    rate_limit_providers_reset: u32,\n}\n\nimpl MendSummary {\n    /// Whether mend performed work that changes bead store state.\n    ///\n    /// Only operations that add or release claimable beads should return true.\n    /// A strand MUST return `WorkCreated` only when it inserts a claimable bead\n    /// into the store that the waterfall will re-scan. No-op cleanup (pruning\n    /// traces, removing stale locks, repairing DB) should return `NoWork`.\n    ///\n    /// Operations that return `WorkCreated`:\n    /// - `beads_released > 0`: Orphaned beads were released back to Open status.\n    /// - `deps_cleaned > 0`: Stale dependency links were removed (beads become claimable).\n    ///\n    /// Operations that return `NoWork` (maintenance, not work creation):\n    /// - `locks_removed > 0`: Lock file cleanup (doesn't add beads to queue).\n    /// - `db_repaired`: Doctor repair fixed index corruption (doesn't add beads).\n    /// - `db_rebuilt`: Full rebuild from JSONL (doesn't add beads).\n    /// - `traces_pruned`, `agent_logs_cleaned`, `learnings_*`: File cleanup.\n    ///\n    /// A `WorkCreated` return must be paired with a telemetry event identifying\n    /// the created bead(s) so operators can see what the restart is chasing.\n    fn did_work(&self) -> bool {\n        // Bead release and dependency removal add claimable items to the queue.\n        // Lock removal, DB repair, and DB rebuild are maintenance operations\n        // that don't create new work and must not trigger a waterfall restart.\n        self.beads_released > 0 || self.deps_cleaned > 0\n    }\n}\n\n/// The Mend strand — maintenance and self-healing.\npub struct MendStrand {\n    config: MendConfig,\n    heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n    heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n    lock_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Fully-qualified worker identity ({adapter}-{worker_id}).\n    /// Used for heartbeat file lookups and registry comparisons to prevent\n    /// collisions when workers from different adapter pools share a NATO name.\n    qualified_id: String,\n    registry: Registry,\n    telemetry: Telemetry,\n    log_dir: PathBuf,\n    retention_days: u32,\n    traces_dir: PathBuf,\n    trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n    trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n    workspace: PathBuf,\n    max_learnings: usize,\n    /// Base state directory (contains `rate_limits/` subdirectory).\n    state_dir: PathBuf,\n    /// Provider/model limits configuration for rate limiter cleanup.\n    limits_config: LimitsConfig,\n}\n\nimpl MendStrand {\n    /// Create a new MendStrand.\n    ///\n    /// - `config`: mend strand configuration\n    /// - `heartbeat_dir`: path to `~/.needle/state/heartbeats/`\n    /// - `heartbeat_ttl`: how long before a - `registry`: worker state registry\n    /// - `telemetry`: telemetry emitter\n    /// - `log_dir`: directory where agent log files live\n    /// - `retention_days`: number of days to retain agent log files (0 = disabled)\n    /// - `traces_dir`: directory where trace files live (`.beads/traces`)\n    /// - `trace_retention_failed_days`: retention days for failed bead traces\n    /// - entries before consolidation\n    /// - `state_dir`: base state directory (contains `rate_limits/` subdirectory)\n    /// - `limits_config`: provider/model limits configuration for rate limiter cleanup\n    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]\n    pub fn new(\n        config: MendConfig,\n        heartbeat_dir: PathBuf,\n        heartbeat_ttl: Duration,\n        lock_dir: PathBuf,\n        qualified_id: String,\n        registry: Registry,\n        telemetry: Telemetry,\n        log_dir: PathBuf,\n        retention_days: u32,\n        traces_dir: PathBuf,\n        trace_retention_failed_days: u32,\n        trace_retention_success_days: u32,\n        workspace: PathBuf,\n        max_learnings: usize,\n        state_dir: PathBuf,\n        limits_config: LimitsConfig,\n    ) -> Self {\n        MendStrand {\n            config,\n            heartbeat_dir,\n            heartbeat_ttl,\n            lock_dir,\n            qualified_id,\n            registry,\n            telemetry,\n            log_dir,\n            retention_days,\n            traces_dir,\n            trace_retention_failed_days,\n            trace_retention_success_days,\n            workspace,\n            max_learnings,\n            state_dir,\n            limits_config,\n        }\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring ──────────────────────\n\n    /// Check for crashed workers and release their orphaned beads.\n    async fn cleanup_stale_claims(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let peer_monitor = PeerMonitor::new(\n            self.heartbeat_dir.clone(),\n            self.heartbeat_ttl,\n            self.qualified_id.clone(),\n            store,\n            &self.registry,\n            self.telemetry.clone(),\n        );\n\n        let peer_result = peer_monitor.check_peers().await?;\n        summary.beads_released += peer_result.beads_released;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery ────────────────────────\n\n    /// Scan all in-progress beads and release any whose assignee does not\n    /// correspond to a live worker. This catches beads orphaned by workers\n    /// that died without leaving a heartbeat file (or whose heartbeat was\n    /// already cleaned up).\n    async fn cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let released = super::mend::cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(\n            store,\n            &self.registry,\n            &self.telemetry,\n            &self.qualified_id,\n        )\n        .await?;\n        summary.beads_released += released;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 1.75: Orphaned heartbeat file removal ────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Remove heartbeat files that have no matching entry in the worker registry.\n    ///\n    /// This can happen due to:\n    /// - Manual deletion of registry entries\n    /// - Registry corruption\n    /// - Worker crash between registry write and heartbeat file creation\n    ///\n    /// Only removes heartbeat files older than heartbeat_ttl to avoid deleting\n    /// recently orphaned files that might still be in use.\n    fn cleanup_orphaned_heartbeats(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        // Read all heartbeat files.\n        let heartbeats = match HealthMonitor::read_all_heartbeats(&self.heartbeat_dir) {\n            Ok(h) => h,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read heartbeat directory for orphaned heartbeat cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Get all registered worker IDs.\n        let registered_ids = match self.registry.list() {\n            Ok(workers) => {\n                let mut ids = std::collections::HashSet::new();\n                for w in workers {\n                    ids.insert(w.id);\n                }\n                ids\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read worker registry for orphaned heartbeat cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for hb in heartbeats {\n            // Use qualified_id for comparison; fall back to worker_id for old heartbeats.\n            let worker_key = if hb.qualified_id.is_empty() {\n                &hb.worker_id\n            } else {\n                &hb.qualified_id\n            };\n\n            // Skip if this heartbeat has a matching registry entry.\n            if registered_ids.contains(worker_key) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip our own heartbeat (shouldn't happen, but be safe).\n            if worker_key == &self.qualified_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if the heartbeat is stale (older than TTL).\n            // Only remove stale heartbeats to avoid deleting recently orphaned files.\n            if !HealthMonitor::is_stale(&hb, self.heartbeat_ttl) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Heartbeat is orphaned and stale — remove it.\n            let heartbeat_path = hb\n                .heartbeat_file\n                .clone()\n                .unwrap_or_else(|| self.heartbeat_dir.join(format!(\"{}.json\", worker_key)));\n\n            let age_secs = match file_age(&heartbeat_path) {\n                Some(age) => age.as_secs(),\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            match std::fs::remove_file(&heartbeat_path) {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        worker_id = %worker_key,\n                        path = %heartbeat_path.display(),\n                        age_secs,\n                        \"removed orphaned heartbeat file\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self\n                        .telemetry\n                        .emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedHeartbeatRemoved {\n                            worker_id: worker_key.clone(),\n                            age_secs,\n                        });\n\n                    summary.orphaned_heartbeats_removed += 1;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        path = %heartbeat_path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to remove orphaned heartbeat file\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal ───────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Remove claim lock files that are not actively held by any process.\n    ///\n    /// This function immediately cleans up lock files whose holding process\n    /// has died, without waiting for an age-based timeout. The flock(2) lock\n    /// is automatically released by the kernel when the holder process dies,\n    /// so try_acquire_flock() will succeed immediately and we can remove the\n    /// stale metadata file.\n    ///\n    /// Lock files older than lock_ttl_secs are also cleaned up as a fallback\n    /// to handle edge cases where the flock probe might fail.\n    fn cleanup_orphaned_locks(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        let lock_ttl = Duration::from_secs(self.config.lock_ttl_secs);\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.lock_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    dir = %self.lock_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to read lock directory\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in entries {\n            let entry = match entry {\n                Ok(e) => e,\n                Err(_) => continue,\n            };\n\n            let path = entry.path();\n            let name = match path.file_name().and_then(|n| n.to_str()) {\n                Some(n) => n,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Only consider needle claim lock files.\n            if !name.starts_with(\"needle-claim-\") || !name.ends_with(\".lock\") {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check file age for logging/fallback cleanup.\n            let age = match file_age(&path) {\n                Some(age) => age,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            // Try to acquire flock (non-blocking). If we can acquire it,\n            // no one is holding it — safe to delete immediately.\n            //\n            // The kernel releases flocks when the holder process dies, so this\n            // probe succeeds immediately for dead PIDs regardless of file age.\n            match try_acquire_flock(&path) {\n                Ok(Some(_file)) => {\n                    // Lock acquired — no one holds it. Remove the file.\n                    let age_secs = age.as_secs();\n                    if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(&path) {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            path = %path.display(),\n                            error = %e,\n                            \"failed to remove orphaned lock file\"\n                        );\n                        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendLockRemoveFailed {\n                            lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                            error: e.to_string(),\n                        });\n                        continue;\n                    }\n\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        age_secs,\n                        \"removed orphaned lock file\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendOrphanedLockRemoved {\n                        lock_path: path.display().to_string(),\n                        age_secs,\n                    });\n\n                    summary.locks_removed += 1;\n                }\n                Ok(None) => {\n                    // Lock is actively held — skip.","numLines":500,"startLine":1,"totalLines":3241}}}

}\n\n    // ── Step 3: Dependency link repair ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Find open beads that have closed blockers and clean up the stale\n    /// dependency links.\n    ///\n    /// br does not automatically resolve dependency links on bead closure,\n    /// so a bead can remain blocked even after its blocker is closed.\n    async fn cleanup_stale_dependencies(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        let all_beads = store.list_all().await?;\n\n        for bead in &all_beads {\n            // Only check open beads that have dependencies.\n            if bead.status != BeadStatus::Open || bead.dependencies.is_empty() {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            for dep in &bead.dependencies {\n                // Only check \"blocks\" type dependencies where the blocker is closed.\n                if dep.dependency_type != \"blocks\" {\n                    continue;\n                }\n\n                if dep.status == \"closed\" {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        bead_id = %bead.id,\n                        blocker_id = %dep.id,\n                        \"removing stale dependency link (closed blocker on open bead)\"\n                    );\n\n                    // Remove the stale dependency link.\n                    match store.remove_dependency(&bead.id, &dep.id).await {\n                        Ok(()) => {\n                            // Emit telemetry for the removed dependency.\n                            let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDependencyRemoved {\n                                bead_id: bead.id.clone(),\n                                blocker_id: dep.id.clone(),\n                            });\n                            summary.deps_cleaned += 1;\n                        }\n                        Err(e) => {\n                            tracing::warn!(\n registry cleanup ───────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Scan the worker registry and remove entries for dead PIDs.\n    ///\n    /// This proactive cleanup ensures that dead worker entries don't accumulate\n    /// in the registry. The registry's list() method already filters dead PIDs,\n    /// but that only happens when rate limit checks run. This step ensures\n    /// cleanup happens every mend cycle.\n    fn cleanup_dead_workers(&self, summary: &mut MendSummary) -> Result<()> {\n        // Read the raw registry file to find entries that need cleanup.\n        let raw_content = match std::fs::read_to_string(self.registry.path()) {\n            Ok(c) => c,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to read registry file\");\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        let raw_reg: crate::registry::RegistryFile = match serde_json::from_str(&raw_content) {\n            Ok(r) => r,\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"failed to parse registry file\");\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        for entry in &raw_reg.workers {\n            // Skip our own entry.\n            if entry.id == self.qualified_id {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Check if the PID is dead.\n            if crate::registry::is_pid_alive(entry.pid) {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Dead worker found — deregister it.\n            match self.registry.deregister(&entry.id) {\n                Ok(()) => {\n                    tracing::info!(\n                        worker_id = %entry.id,\n                        pid = entry.pid,\n                        \"removed dead worker from registry\"\n                    );\n\n                    let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendWorkerDeregistered {\n                        worker_id: entry.id.clone(),\n                        pid: entry.pid,\n                    });\n\n                    summary.workers_deregistered += 1;\n                }\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        worker_id = %entry.id,\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to deregister dead worker\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 4: Agent log file ///\n    /// Files are matched by the `.agent.jsonl` suffix. The bead ID is parsed\n    /// from the stem (`<worker>-<bead>`): a file is considered active if the\n    /// stem ends with `-<bead_id>` for any in-progress bead.\n    async fn cleanup_old_agent_logs(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        if self.retention_days == 0 {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        let entries = match std::fs::read_dir(&self.log_dir) {\n            Ok(e) => e,\n            Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => return Ok(()),\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    dir = %self.log_dir.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to read log directory for agent log cleanup\"\n                );\n                return Ok(());\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Collect all .agent.jsonl paths first so we can bail early if none.\n        let agent_log_paths:                  .map(|n| n.ends_with(\".agent.jsonl\"))\n                    .unwrap_or(false)\n            })\n            .collect();\n\n        if agent_log_paths.is_empty() {\n            return std::collections::HashSet<String> = store\n            .list_all()\n            .await?\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|b| b.status == BeadStatus::InProgress)\n            .map(|b| b.id.as_ref().to_string())\n            .collect();\n\n        for path in &agent_log_paths {\n            // Check file age.\n            let age = match file_age(path) {\n                Some(a) => a,\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            if age <= cutoff {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip if this log belongs to an in-progress bead.\n Some(s) => s.to_string(),\n                None => continue,\n            };\n\n            let is_active = in_progress_ids\n                .iter()\n                .any(|bead_id| stem == *bead_id || stem.ends_with(&format!(\"-{bead_id}\")));\n\n            if is_active {\n                tracing::debug!(\n                    path = %path.display(),\n                    \"skipping agent log for in-progress bead\"\n                );\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            if let Err(e) = std::fs::remove_file(path) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    path = %path.display(),\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"mend: failed to remove stale agent log\"\n                );\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            tracing::info!(\n                path = %path.display(),\n                age_secs = age.as_secs(),\n                \"mend: removed stale agent log\"\n            );\n            summary.agent_logs_cleaned += 1;\n        }\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup ───────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Clean up old traces based on           Ok(c) => c,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        provider,\n                        path = %path.display(),\n TokenBucket = match serde_json::from_str(&content) {\n                Ok(b) => b,\n                Err(e) => {\n                    tracing::debug!(\n                        provider,\n                        path = %path.display(),\n                        error = %e,\n                        \"failed to parse rate limit state file\"\n                    );\n                    continue;\n check database health. If issues are found,\n    /// escalate through the recovery pipeline:\n    ///\n    /// 1. `br doctor` (check only) → if warnings found:\n    /// 2. `br doctor --repair` → if fails:\n    /// 3. Full rebuild (rm .beads/beads.db + br sync --import + verify)\n    ///\n    /// If the full rebuild also fails, the JSONL itself may be corrupt.\n    async fn check_db_health(\n        &self,\n        store: &dyn BeadStore,\n        summary: &mut MendSummary,\n    ) -> Result<()> {\n        // Step 1: Probe with `br doctor` (no repair).\n        let check_result = store.doctor_check().await;\n\n        let needs_repair = match check_result {\n            Ok(report) => {\n                // Doctor succeeded. If there are warnings, escalate to repair.\n                !report.warnings.is_empty()\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                let msg = format!(\"{e:#}\");\n                if crate::bead_store::is_corruption_error(&msg) {\n                    tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"br doctor detected corruption\");\n                    true\n                } else {\n                    // Non-corruption error (e.g., br not found). Propagate.\n                    return Err(e);\n                }\n            }\n        };\n\n        if !needs_repair {\n            return Ok(());\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Try `br doctor --repair`.\n        tracing::info!(\"database health check found issues, attempting repair\");\n        match store.doctor_repair().await {\n            Ok(report) => {\n                let warnings = report.warnings.len() as u32;\n                let fixed = report.fixed.len() as u32;\n                tracing::info!(warnings, fixed, \"br doctor --repair completed\");\n\n                let _ = self\n                    .telemetry\n                    .emit(EventKind::MendDbRepaired { warnings, fixed });\n\n                // Only mark as repaired if actual fixes were applied.\n                // If warnings persist without fixes, returning WorkCreated would\n                // cause an infinite restart loop without making progress.\n                if fixed > 0 {\n                    summary.db_repaired = true;\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n\n                // If doctor_repair succeeded but warnings persist without fixes,\n                // escalate to full rebuild. This prevents repeated Mend cycles\n                // where each evaluation calls doctor_repair which returns the\n                // same unfixed warnings without making progress.\n                if !report.warnings.is_empty() {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        warnings,\n                        \"doctor --repair succeeded but warnings persist without fixes, escalating to full rebuild\"\n                    );\n                } else {\n                    // No warnings and no fixes - DB is clean, no work needed.\n                    return Ok(());\n                }\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"br doctor --repair failed, attempting full rebuild\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Step 3: Full rebuild — rm db + br sync --import + verify.\n        match store.full_rebuild().await {\n            Ok(()) => {\n                tracing::info!(\"database fully rebuilt from JSONL\");\n\n                let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendDbRebuilt);\n\n                summary.db_rebuilt = true;\n                Ok(())\n            }\n            Err(e) => {\n                tracing::error!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"full database rebuild failed — JSONL may be corrupt\"\n                );\n                Err(e)\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n#[async_trait::async_trait]\nimpl super::Strand for MendStrand {\n    fn name(&self) -> &str {\n        \"mend\"\n    }\n\n    async fn evaluate(&self, store: &dyn BeadStore) -> StrandResult {\n        let mut summary = MendSummary::default();\n\n        // Step 1: Stale claim cleanup via peer monitoring.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_claims(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: stale claim cleanup failed\");\n            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n        }\n\n        // Step 1.5: Orphaned in-progress bead recovery (registry-based).\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_in_progress(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned in-progress cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 1.75: Orphaned heartbeat file removal.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_heartbeats(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned heartbeat cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 2: Orphaned lock file removal.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_orphaned_locks(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: orphaned lock cleanup failed\");\n            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3: Dependency link repair.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_stale_dependencies(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: dependency cleanup failed\");\n            return StrandResult::Error(StrandError::StoreError(e));\n        }\n\n        // Step 3.5: Dead worker registry cleanup.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_dead_workers(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: dead worker cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4: Agent log file cleanup.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_agent_logs(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: agent log cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.5: Trace retention cleanup.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_old_traces(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: trace cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.75: Learning consolidation.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_learnings(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: learning cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.8: Idle worker flagging.\n        if let Err(e) = self.flag_idle_workers(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: idle worker flagging failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 4.9: Rate limiter state cleanup.\n        if let Err(e) = self.cleanup_rate_limit_state(&mut summary) {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: rate limit state cleanup failed\");\n            // Non-fatal — continue with remaining steps.\n        }\n\n        // Step 5: Database health check.\n        if let Err(e) = self.check_db_health(store, &mut summary).await {\n            tracing::warn!(error = %e, \"mend: database health check failed\");\n            // DB check failure is non-fatal — continue with the summary.\n        }\n\n        // Emit cycle summary telemetry.\n        let _ = self.telemetry.emit(EventKind::MendCycleSummary {\n            beads_released: summary.beads_released,\n            locks_removed: summary.locks_removed,\n            deps_cleaned: summary.deps_cleaned,\n            db_repaired: summary.db_repaired,\n            db_rebuilt: summary.db_rebuilt,\n            agent_logs_cleaned: summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n            traces_pruned: summary.traces_pruned,\n            traces_deleted: summary.traces_cleaned,\n            workers_deregistered: summary.workers_deregistered,\n            idle_workers_flagged: summary.idle_workers_flagged,\n            rate_limits_cleaned: summary.rate_limits_cleaned,\n        });\n\n        if summary.did_work() {\n            tracing::info!(\n                beads_released = summary.beads_released,\n                locks_removed = summary.locks_removed,\n                deps_cleaned = summary.deps_cleaned,\n                db_repaired = summary.db_repaired,\n                db_rebuilt = summary.db_rebuilt,\n                agent_logs_cleaned = summary.agent_logs_cleaned,\n                traces_pruned = summary.traces_pruned,\n                traces_deleted = summary.traces_cleaned,\n                \"mend performed cleanup — restarting waterfall\"\n            );\n            StrandResult::WorkCreated\n        } else {\n            tracing::debug!(\"mend found nothing to clean\");\n            StrandResult::NoWork\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Helpers\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Get the age of a file based on its modification time.\nfn file_age(path: &Path) -> Option<Duration> {\n    let metadata = std::fs::metadata(path).ok()?;\n    let modified = metadata.modified().ok()?;\n    SystemTime::now().duration_since(modified).ok()\n}\n\n/// Try to acquire an exclusive flock on a file (non-blocking).\n///\n/// Returns `Ok(Some(file))` if acquired, `Ok(None)` if held by another process.\nfn try_acquire_flock(path: &Path) -> Result<Option<std::fs::File>> {\n    use fs2::FileExt;\n\n    let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n        .read(true)\n        .write(true)\n        .open(path)?;\n\n    match file.try_lock_exclusive() {\n        Ok(()) => Ok(Some(file)),\n        Err(ref e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::WouldBlock => Ok(None),\n        Err(e) => Err(e.into()),\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// concurrency limits.\n28\tpub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n29\t    #[cfg(unix)]\n30\t    {\n31\t        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n32\t        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n33\t        unsafe {\n34\t            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n35\t            if ret == 0 {\n36\t                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n37\t                return true;\n38\t            }\n39\t            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n40\t            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n41\t            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n42\t            match errno {\n43\t                libc::ESRCH => {\n44\t                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n45\t                    false\n46\t                }\n47\t                libc::EPERM => {\n48\t                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n49\t                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n50\t                    true\n51\t                }\n52\t                _ => {\n53\t                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n54\t                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n55\t                    false\n56\t                }\n57\t            }\n58\t        }\n59\t    }\n60\t\n61\t    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n62\t    {\n63\t        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n64\t        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n65\t        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n66\t        false\n67\t    }\n68\t}\n69\t\n70\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n71\t// WorkerEntry\n72\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n73\t\n74\t/// A single worker entry in the registry.\n75\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]\n76\tpub struct WorkerEntry {\n77\t    /// Fully-qualified worker identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`, e.g., `claude-foxtrot`).\n78\t    pub id: String,\n79\t    /// Process ID of the worker.\n80\t    pub pid: u32,\n81\t    /// Workspace the worker is processing beads from.\n82\t    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n83\t    /// Agent adapter name.\n84\t    pub agent: String,\n85\t    /// Model name (if known).\n86\t    pub model: Option<String>,\n87\t    /// Provider name (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`).\n88\t    pub provider: Option<String>,\n89\t    /// When the worker started.\n90\t    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n91\t    /// Number of beads processed so far.\n92\t    pub beads_processed: u64,\n93\t}\n94\t\n95\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n96\t// RegistryFile\n97\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n98\t\n99\t/// The on-disk JSON structure for `workers.json`.\n100\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n101\tpub struct RegistryFile {\n102\t    pub workers: Vec<WorkerEntry>,\n103\t    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n104\t}\n105\t\n106\timpl Default for RegistryFile {\n107\t    fn default() -> Self {\n108\t        RegistryFile {\n109\t            workers: Vec::new(),\n110\t            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n111\t        }\n112\t    }\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n116\t// Registry\n117\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n118\t\n119\t/// Worker state registry with flock-protected read-modify-write access.\n120\t#[derive(Clone)]\n121\tpub struct Registry {\n122\t    path: PathBuf,\n123\t}\n124\t\n125\timpl Registry {\n126\t    /// Create a registry instance pointing at the given state directory.\n127\t    ///\n128\t    /// The directory will be created on first write if it does not exist.\n129\t    pub fn new(state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n130\t        Registry {\n131\t            path: state_dir.join(\"workers.json\"),\n132\t        }\n133\t    }\n134\t\n135\t    /// Create a registry using the default state directory (`~/.needle/state`).\n136\t    pub fn default_location(needle_home: &Path) -> Self {\n137\t        Self::new(&needle_home.join(\"state\"))\n138\t    }\n139\t\n140\t    /// Path to the workers.json file.\n141\t    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n142\t        &self.path\n143\t    }\n144\t\n145\t    /// Register a worker on startup.\n146\t    ///\n147\t    /// If a worker with the same ID already exists, it is replaced (handles\n148\t    /// stale entries from previous runs).\n149\t    pub fn register(&self, entry: WorkerEntry) -> Result<()> {\n150\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n151\t            // Remove any existing entry with the same ID (stale from previous run).\n152\t            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != entry.id);\n153\t            reg.workers.push(entry);\n154\t        })\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    /// Deregister a worker on shutdown.\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// Best-effort: errors are logged but not propagated to avoid masking\n160\t    /// the real shutdown reason.\n161\t    pub fn deregister(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n162\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n163\t            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != worker_id);\n164\t        })\n165\t    }\n166\t\n167\t    /// Update a worker's beads_processed count.\n168\t    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, worker_id: &str, beads_processed: u64) -> Result<()> {\n169\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n170\t            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n171\t                entry.beads_processed = beads_processed;\n172\t            }\n173\t        })\n174\t    }\n175\t\n176\t    /// Update a worker's current workspace.\n177\t    pub fn update_workspace(&self, worker_id: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n178\t        self.modify(|reg| {\n179\t            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n180\t                entry.workspace = workspace.to_path_buf();\n181\t            }\n182\t        })\n183\t    }\n184\t\n185\t    /// Read all registered workers, filtering out entries for dead PIDs.\n186\t    ///\n187\t    /// This lazy cleanup ensures that workers killed via SIGKILL or crashes\n188\t    /// don't accumulate in the registry and falsely count toward concurrency\n189\t    /// limits.\n190\t    pub fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerEntry>> {\n191\t        let reg = self.read()?;\n192\t        let total_count = reg.workers.len();\n193\t        let live_workers: Vec<WorkerEntry> = reg\n194\t            .workers\n195\t            .into_iter()\n196\t            .filter(|w| is_pid_alive(w.pid))\n197\t            .collect();\n198\t\n199\t        // If we filtered out dead entries, persist the cleanup so we don't\n200\t        // need to re-check their PIDs on every read. This is best-effort:\n201\t        // if the write fails (disk full, race with another writer), we still\n202\t        // return the correctly filtered list — we'll just re-filter next time.\n203\t        let live_count = live_workers.len();\n204\t        if live_count != total_count {\n205\t            let dead_count = total_count - live_count;\n206\t            tracing::debug!(dead_count, \"filtered dead worker entries from registry\");\n207\t\n208\t            let cleaned_reg = RegistryFile {\n209\t                workers: live_workers.clone(),\n210\t                updated_at: Utc::now(),\n211\t            };\n212\t            if let Err(e) = self.write_cleaned(cleaned_reg) {\n213\t                tracing::warn!(\n214\t                    error = %e,\n215\t                    \"failed to persist dead PID cleanup; will re-filter next read\"\n216\t                );\n217\t            }\n218\t        }\n219\t\n220\t        Ok(live_workers)\n221\t    }\n222\t\n223\t    /// Write a cleaned registry file (used by list() to persist dead PID filtering).\n224\t    ///\n225\t    /// No file locking: read() already released its shared lock before returning.\n226\t    /// The atomic rename ensures concurrent writers don't corrupt the file —\n227\t    /// one write wins cleanly.\n228\t    fn write_cleaned(&self, reg: RegistryFile) -> Result<()> {\n229\t        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n230\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n231\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n232\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n233\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n234\t            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n235\t        })?;\n236\t        Ok(())\n237\t    }\n238\t\n239\t    // ── Internal helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n240\t\n241\t    /// Read the registry file, returning a default if it doesn't exist.\n242\t    fn read(&self) -> Result<RegistryFile> {\n243\t        if !self.path.exists() {\n244\t            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n245\t        }\n246\t\n247\t        let file = std::fs::File::open(&self.path)\n248\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n249\t\n250\t        // Shared lock for reading (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n251\t        FileExt::lock_shared(&file)\n252\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to acquire shared lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n253\t\n254\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path)\n255\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n256\t\n257\t        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n258\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n259\t\n260\t        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n261\t            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n262\t        }\n263\t\n264\t        serde_json::from_str(&content)\n265\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse registry JSON: {}\", self.path.display()))\n266\t    }\n267\t\n268\t    /// Perform a flock-protected read-modify-write operation.\n269\t    fn modify<F>(&self, mutator: F) -> Result<()>\n270\t    where\n271\t        F: FnOnce(&mut RegistryFile),\n272\t    {\n273\t        // Ensure the parent directory exists.\n274\t        if let Some(parent) = self.path.parent() {\n275\t            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).with_context(|| {\n276\t                format!(\"failed to create registry directory: {}\", parent.display())\n277\t            })?;\n278\t        }\n279\t\n280\t        // Open or create the file.\n281\t        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n282\t            .read(true)\n283\t            .write(true)\n284\t            .create(true)\n285\t            .truncate(false)\n286\t            .open(&self.path)\n287\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n288\t\n289\t        // Exclusive lock for writing (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n290\t        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n291\t            format!(\"failed to acquire exclusive lock: {}\", self.path.display())\n292\t        })?;\n293\t\n294\t        // Read current contents.\n295\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path).unwrap_or_default();\n296\t        let mut reg: RegistryFile = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n297\t            RegistryFile::default()\n298\t        } else {\n299\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default()\n300\t        };\n301\t\n302\t        // Apply the mutation.\n303\t        mutator(&mut reg);\n304\t        reg.updated_at = Utc::now();\n305\t\n306\t        // Write back atomically: write to temp file, then rename.\n307\t        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n308\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n309\t        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n310\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n311\t        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n312\t            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n313\t        })?;\n314\t\n315\t        // Release lock (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n316\t        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n317\t            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n318\t\n319\t        Ok(())\n320\t    }\n321\t}\n322\t\n323\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n324\t// Tests\n325\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n326\t\n327\t#[cfg(test)]\n328\tmod tests {\n329\t    use super::*;\n330\t\n331\t    fn make_entry(id: &str) ->   fn is_pid_alive_returns_true_for_current_process() {\n553\t        // The current process's PID should be alive.\n554\t        assert!(is_pid_alive(std::process::id()));\n555\t    }\n556\t\n557\t    #[test]\n558\t    fn is_pid_alive_returns_false_for_nonexistent_pid() {\n559\t        // A PID that's unlikely to exist should be dead.\n560\t        // DEAD_PID is well beyond any valid PID on real
