}\n563\t}\n564\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"be91b0f0-a74a-4db6-99c2-a69ec602ba4d","uuid":"661c276c-9866-4287-847f-f51822c8dcbb","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:35:59.689Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/registry/mod.rs","content":"//! Worker state registry — shared JSON file tracking all active workers.\n//!\n//! The registry is informational: \"who is running, what are they doing, how\n//! many beads processed.\" It is NOT used for coordination — heartbeats handle\n//! that.\n//!\n//! File: `~/.needle/state/workers.json`\n//! Access: flock-protected read-modify-write (atomic updates).\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse fs2::FileExt;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// PID liveness checking (platform-specific)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Check if a process with the given PID is currently running.\n///\n/// Returns `false` if the PID does not exist or if we lack permission to signal it.\n/// This is best-effort: if we can't determine liveness, we assume the process is dead\n/// to avoid counting stale entries toward concurrency limits.\npub fn is_pid_alive(pid: u32) -> bool {\n    #[cfg(unix)]\n    {\n        // Use libc::kill with signal 0 to check if process exists.\n        // SAFETY: kill(pid, 0) only checks existence, doesn't send a signal.\n        unsafe {\n            let ret = libc::kill(pid as i32, 0);\n            if ret == 0 {\n                // kill succeeded: process exists and we have permission to signal it.\n                return true;\n            }\n            // kill failed: check errno to distinguish EPERM from ESRCH.\n            // We must read errno immediately after kill, before any other syscalls.\n            let errno = *libc::__errno_location();\n            match errno {\n                libc::ESRCH => {\n                    // No such process: PID is dead.\n                    false\n                }\n                libc::EPERM => {\n                    // Process exists but we don't have permission to signal it.\n                    // We treat this as \"alive\" since the process actually exists.\n                    true\n                }\n                _ => {\n                    // Other errors (e.g., EINVAL for invalid signal).\n                    // Conservatively treat as dead to avoid false positives.\n                    false\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[cfg(not(unix))]\n    {\n        // On non-Unix platforms, conservatively return false (assume dead).\n        // This prevents false positives where dead PIDs are counted as live.\n        // TODO: Implement Windows liveness check via OpenProcess if needed.\n        false\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// WorkerEntry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// A single worker entry in the registry.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize, PartialEq)]\npub struct WorkerEntry {\n    /// Fully-qualified worker identity (`{adapter}-{worker_id}`, e.g., `claude-foxtrot`).\n    pub id: String,\n    /// Process ID of the worker.\n    pub pid: u32,\n    /// Workspace the worker is processing beads from.\n    pub workspace: PathBuf,\n    /// Agent adapter name.\n    pub agent: String,\n    /// Model name (if known).\n    pub model: Option<String>,\n    /// Provider name (e.g., `anthropic`, `openai`).\n    pub provider: Option<String>,\n    /// When the worker started.\n    pub started_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n    /// Number of beads processed so far.\n    pub beads_processed: u64,\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// RegistryFile\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// The on-disk JSON structure for `workers.json`.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct RegistryFile {\n    pub workers: Vec<WorkerEntry>,\n    pub updated_at: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl Default for RegistryFile {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        RegistryFile {\n            workers: Vec::new(),\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Registry\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Worker state registry with flock-protected read-modify-write access.\n#[derive(Clone)]\npub struct Registry {\n    path: PathBuf,\n}\n\nimpl Registry {\n    /// Create a registry instance pointing at the given state directory.\n    ///\n    /// The directory will be created on first write if it does not exist.\n    pub fn new(state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n        Registry {\n            path: state_dir.join(\"workers.json\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a registry using the default state directory (`~/.needle/state`).\n    pub fn default_location(needle_home: &Path) -> Self {\n        Self::new(&needle_home.join(\"state\"))\n    }\n\n    /// Path to the workers.json file.\n    pub fn path(&self) -> &Path {\n        &self.path\n    }\n\n    /// Register a worker on startup.\n    ///\n    /// If a worker with the same ID already exists, it is replaced (handles\n    /// stale entries from previous runs).\n    pub fn register(&self, entry: WorkerEntry) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            // Remove any existing entry with the same ID (stale from previous run).\n            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != entry.id);\n            reg.workers.push(entry);\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Deregister a worker on shutdown.\n    ///\n    /// Best-effort: errors are logged but not propagated to avoid masking\n    /// the real shutdown reason.\n    pub fn deregister(&self, worker_id: &str) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            reg.workers.retain(|w| w.id != worker_id);\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Update a worker's beads_processed count.\n    pub fn update_beads_processed(&self, worker_id: &str, beads_processed: u64) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n                entry.beads_processed = beads_processed;\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Update a worker's current workspace.\n    pub fn update_workspace(&self, worker_id: &str, workspace: &Path) -> Result<()> {\n        self.modify(|reg| {\n            if let Some(entry) = reg.workers.iter_mut().find(|w| w.id == worker_id) {\n                entry.workspace = workspace.to_path_buf();\n            }\n        })\n    }\n\n    /// Read all registered workers, filtering out entries for dead PIDs.\n    ///\n    /// This lazy cleanup ensures that workers killed via SIGKILL or crashes\n    /// don't accumulate in the registry and falsely count toward concurrency\n    /// limits.\n    pub fn list(&self) -> Result<Vec<WorkerEntry>> {\n        let reg = self.read()?;\n        let total_count = reg.workers.len();\n        let live_workers: Vec<WorkerEntry> = reg\n            .workers\n            .into_iter()\n            .filter(|w| is_pid_alive(w.pid))\n            .collect();\n\n        // If we filtered out dead entries, persist the cleanup so we don't\n        // need to re-check their PIDs on every read. This is best-effort:\n        // if the write fails (disk full, race with another writer), we still\n        // return the correctly filtered list — we'll just re-filter next time.\n        let live_count = live_workers.len();\n        if live_count != total_count {\n            let dead_count = total_count - live_count;\n            tracing::debug!(dead_count, \"filtered dead worker entries from registry\");\n\n            let cleaned_reg = RegistryFile {\n                workers: live_workers.clone(),\n                updated_at: Utc::now(),\n            };\n            if let Err(e) = self.write_cleaned(cleaned_reg) {\n                tracing::warn!(\n                    error = %e,\n                    \"failed to persist dead PID cleanup; will re-filter next read\"\n                );\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(live_workers)\n    }\n\n    /// Write a cleaned registry file (used by list() to persist dead PID filtering).\n    ///\n    /// No file locking: read() already released its shared lock before returning.\n    /// The atomic rename ensures concurrent writers don't corrupt the file —\n    /// one write wins cleanly.\n    fn write_cleaned(&self, reg: RegistryFile) -> Result<()> {\n        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n        Ok(())\n    }\n\n    // ── Internal helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n    /// Read the registry file, returning a default if it doesn't exist.\n    fn read(&self) -> Result<RegistryFile> {\n        if !self.path.exists() {\n            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n        }\n\n        let file = std::fs::File::open(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        // Shared lock for reading (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::lock_shared(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to acquire shared lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to read registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            return Ok(RegistryFile::default());\n        }\n\n        serde_json::from_str(&content)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to parse registry JSON: {}\", self.path.display()))\n    }\n\n    /// Perform a flock-protected read-modify-write operation.\n    fn modify<F>(&self, mutator: F) -> Result<()>\n    where\n        F: FnOnce(&mut RegistryFile),\n    {\n        // Ensure the parent directory exists.\n        if let Some(parent) = self.path.parent() {\n            std::fs::create_dir_all(parent).with_context(|| {\n                format!(\"failed to create registry directory: {}\", parent.display())\n            })?;\n        }\n\n        // Open or create the file.\n        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n            .read(true)\n            .write(true)\n            .create(true)\n            .truncate(false)\n            .open(&self.path)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to open registry: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        // Exclusive lock for writing (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to acquire exclusive lock: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n\n        // Read current contents.\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&self.path).unwrap_or_default();\n        let mut reg: RegistryFile = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            RegistryFile::default()\n        } else {\n            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default()\n        };\n\n        // Apply the mutation.\n        mutator(&mut reg);\n        reg.updated_at = Utc::now();\n\n        // Write back atomically: write to temp file, then rename.\n        let tmp_path = self.path.with_extension(\"json.tmp\");\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg).context(\"failed to serialize registry\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&tmp_path, &json)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to write temp registry: {}\", tmp_path.display()))?;\n        std::fs::rename(&tmp_path, &self.path).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\"failed to rename temp registry to: {}\", self.path.display())\n        })?;\n\n        // Release lock (use fs2 trait method explicitly for MSRV compat).\n        FileExt::unlock(&file)\n            .with_context(|| format!(\"failed to release lock: {}\", self.path.display()))?;\n\n        Ok(())\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    fn make_entry(id: &str) -> WorkerEntry {\n        WorkerEntry {\n            id: id.to_string(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test-workspace\"),\n            agent: \"claude\".to_string(),\n            model: Some(\"sonnet\".to_string()),\n            provider: Some(\"anthropic\".to_string()),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// A PID that is definitely not in use on any real system.\n    ///\n    /// This must be a value that:\n    /// 1. Is positive when cast to i32 (avoid -1 special case)\n    /// 2. Is unlikely to ever be assigned by the kernel\n    ///\n    /// We use 9999999 which is well beyond typical PID ranges.\n    const DEAD_PID: u32 = 9999999;\n\n    #[test]\n    fn register_and_list() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"bravo\")).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 2);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"alpha\");\n        assert_eq!(workers[1].id, \"bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn deregister_removes_worker() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"bravo\")).unwrap();\n        reg.deregister(\"alpha\").unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn deregister_nonexistent_is_noop() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.deregister(\"nonexistent\").unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn register_replaces_stale_entry() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        let mut entry1 = make_entry(\"alpha\");\n        entry1.beads_processed = 5;\n        reg.register(entry1).unwrap();\n\n        let entry2 = make_entry(\"alpha\");\n        reg.register(entry2).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].beads_processed, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn update_beads_processed() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.update_beads_processed(\"alpha\", 42).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].beads_processed, 42);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn update_nonexistent_worker_is_noop() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n        reg.update_beads_processed(\"ghost\", 10).unwrap();\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].beads_processed, 0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn empty_registry_returns_empty_list() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert!(workers.is_empty());\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_file_is_valid_json() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"alpha\")).unwrap();\n\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(reg.path()).unwrap();\n        let parsed: RegistryFile = serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers.len(), 1);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn default_location_uses_state_subdir() {\n        let reg = Registry::default_location(Path::new(\"/home/test/.needle\"));\n        assert_eq!(\n            reg.path(),\n            Path::new(\"/home/test/.needle/state/workers.json\")\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn concurrent_registration_no_corruption() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n\n        // Simulate sequential registrations (true concurrency tested in integration tests).\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        for i in 0..10 {\n            reg.register(make_entry(&format!(\"worker-{i}\"))).unwrap();\n        }\n\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 10);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn registry_json_roundtrip() {\n        let entry = make_entry(\"alpha\");\n        let reg_file = RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![entry.clone()],\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        };\n\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&reg_file).unwrap();\n        let parsed: RegistryFile = serde_json::from_str(&json).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers[0].id, entry.id);\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers[0].pid, entry.pid);\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers[0].agent, entry.agent);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn list_filters_out_dead_pids() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        // Register a live worker (current process).\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"live-alpha\")).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an entry with a fake (dead) PID.\n        let mut dead_entry = make_entry(\"dead-worker\");\n        dead_entry.pid = DEAD_PID;\n\n        // Write the dead entry directly to the file.\n        let file_content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![make_entry(\"live-alpha\"), dead_entry],\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(reg.path(), file_content).unwrap();\n\n        // list() should filter out the dead PID.\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(workers[0].id, \"live-alpha\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn list_persists_cleanup_after_filtering_dead_pids() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let reg = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        // Register a live worker.\n        reg.register(make_entry(\"live-bravo\")).unwrap();\n\n        // Create an entry with a fake (dead) PID.\n        let mut dead_entry = make_entry(\"dead-charlie\");\n        dead_entry.pid = DEAD_PID;\n\n        // Write both entries to the file.\n        let file_content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![make_entry(\"live-bravo\"), dead_entry],\n            updated_at: Utc::now(),\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(reg.path(), file_content).unwrap();\n\n        // list() should filter and persist the cleanup.\n        let workers = reg.list().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(workers.len(), 1);\n\n        // Read the file directly to verify cleanup was persisted.\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(reg.path()).unwrap();\n        let parsed: RegistryFile = serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers.len(), 1);\n        assert_eq!(parsed.workers[0].id, \"live-bravo\");\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn is_pid_alive_returns_true_for_current_process() {\n        // The current process's PID should be alive.\n        assert!(is_pid_alive(std::process::id()));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn is_pid_alive_returns_false_for_nonexistent_pid() {\n        // A PID that's unlikely to exist should be dead.\n        // DEAD_PID is well beyond any valid PID on real systems.\n        assert!(!is_pid_alive(DEAD_PID));\n    }\n}\n","numLines":564,"startLine":1,"totalLines":564}}}

Before dispatching an agent, the worker checks:\n4\t//! 1. **Concurrency limits**: Are fewer than `max_concurrent` workers using\n5\t//!    this provider/model? Checked via the worker registry.\n6\t//! 2. **RPM limits**: Has the provider's requests-per-minute budget been\n7\t//!    exceeded? Checked via a file-based token bucket.\n8\t//!\n9\t//! If either check fails, the caller should back off and retry.\n10\t//!\n11\t//! ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n26\t\n27\t/// Result of a rate limit check.\n28\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\n29\tpub enum RateLimitDecision {\n30\t    /// Dispatch is allowed.\n31\t    Allowed,\n32\t    /// Provider concurrency limit reached.\n33\t    ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n34\t        provider: String,\n35\t        current: RateLimitDecision {\n52\t    /// Whether dispatch is allowed.\n53\t    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n54\t        matches!(self, RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n55\t    }\n56\t}\n57\t\n58\timpl std::fmt::Display for RateLimitDecision {\n59\t    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n60\t        match self {\n61\t            RateLimitDecision::Allowed => (file-based)\n94\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n95\t\n96\t/// On-disk token bucket state for RPM limiting.\n97\t#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\n98\tpub struct TokenBucket {\n99\t    /// Current available tokens (fractional for smooth refill).\n100\t    pub tokens: f64,\n101\t    /// Maximum tokens (= requests_per_minute).\n102\t    pub capacity: u32,\n103\t    /// Last time tokens were refilled.\n104\t    pub last_refill: DateTime<Utc>,\n105\t}\n106\t\n107\timpl TokenBucket {\n108\t    pub(crate) fn new(capacity: u32) -> Self {\n109\t        TokenBucket {\n110\t            tokens: capacity as f64,\n111\t            capacity,\n112\t            last_refill: Utc::now(),\n113\t        }\n114\t    }\n115\t\n116\t    /// Refill tokens based on elapsed time since last refill.\n117\t    fn refill(&mut self) {\n118\t        let now = Utc::now();\n119\t        let elapsed_secs = (now - self.last_refill).num_milliseconds().max(0) as f64 / 1000.0;\n120\t        let refill_rate = self.capacity as f64 / 60.0; // tokens per second\n121\t        self.tokens = (self.tokens + elapsed_secs * refill_rate).min(self.capacity as f64);\n122\t        self.last_refill = now;\n123\t    }\n124\t\n125\t    /// Try to consume one token. Returns true if successful.\n126\t    fn try_consume(&mut self) -> bool {\n127\t        self.refill();\n128\t        if self.tokens >= 1.0 {\n129\t            self.tokens -= 1.0;\n130\t            true\n131\t        } else {\n132\t            false\n133\t        }\n134\t    }\n135\t}\n136\t\n137\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n138\t// RateLimiter\n139\t// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n140\t\n141\t/// Enforces provider/model concurrency limits and RPM rate limiting.\n142\tpub struct config,\n153\t            state_dir: state_dir.join(\"rate_limits\"),\n154\t        }\n155\t    }\n156\t\n157\t    /// Check all rate limits before dispatching.\n158\t    ///\n159\t    /// Returns `Allowed` if dispatch can proceed, or a specific reason why not.\n160\t    /// The caller should back off and retry if not allowed.\n161\t    pub fn check(\n162\t        &self,\n163\t        provider: Option<&str>,\n164\t        model: Option<&str>,\n165\t        registry: &Registry,\n166\t    ) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n167\t        // Check provider concurrency.\n168\t        if let Some(provider_name) = provider {\n169\t            if let Some(provider_limits) = self.config.providers.get(provider_name) {\n170\t                if let Some(max_concurrent) = provider_limits.max_concurrent {\n171\t                    let decision =\n172\t                        self.check_provider_concurrency(provider_name, max_concurrent, registry)?;\n173\t                    if !decision.is_allowed() {\n174\t                        return Ok(decision);\n175\t                    }\n176\t                }\n177\t            }\n178\t        }\n179\t\n180\t        // Check model concurrency.\n181\t        if let Some(model_name) = model {\n182\t            if let Some(model_limits) = self.config.models.get(model_name) {\n183\t                if let Some(max_concurrent) = model_limits.max_concurrent {\n184\t                    let decision =\n185\t                        self.check_model_concurrency(model_name, max_concurrent, registry)?;\n186\t                    if !decision.is_allowed() {\n187\t                        return Ok(decision);\n188\t                    }\n189\t                }\n190\t            }\n191\t        }\n192\t\n193\t        // Check RPM.\n194\t        if let Some(provider_name) = provider {\n195\t            if let Some(provider_limits) = self.config.providers.get(provider_name) {\n196\t                if let Some(rpm) = provider_limits.requests_per_minute {\n197\t                    let decision = self.check_rpm(provider_name, rpm)?;\n198\t                    if !decision.is_allowed() {\n199\t                        return Ok(decision);\n200\t                    }\n201\t                }\n202\t            }\n203\t        }\n204\t\n205\t        Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n206\t    }\n207\t\n208\t    /// Check provider concurrency against the worker registry.\n209\t    fn check_provider_concurrency(\n210\t        &self,\n211\t        provider: &str,\n212\t        max_concurrent: u32,\n213\t        registry: &Registry,\n214\t    ) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n215\t        let workers = registry.list()?;\n216\t        let active_count = workers\n217\t            .iter()\n218\t            .filter(|w| w.provider.as_deref() == Some(provider))\n219\t            .count() as u32;\n220\t\n221\t        if active_count >= max_concurrent {\n222\t            Ok(RateLimitDecision::ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n223\t                provider: provider.to_string(),\n224\t                current: active_count,\n225\t                limit: max_concurrent,\n226\t            })\n227\t        } else {\n228\t            Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n229\t        }\n230\t    }\n231\t\n232\t    /// Check model concurrency against the worker registry.\n233\t    fn check_model_concurrency(\n234\t        &self,\n235\t        model: &str,\n236\t        max_concurrent: u32,\n237\t        registry: &Registry,\n238\t    ) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n239\t        let workers = registry.list()?;\n240\t        let active_count = workers\n241\t            .iter()\n242\t            .filter(|w| w.model.as_deref() == Some(model))\n243\t            .count() as u32;\n244\t\n245\t        if active_count >= limit: max_concurrent,\n250\t            })\n251\t        } else {\n252\t            Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n253\t        }\n254\t    }\n255\t\n256\t    /// Check RPM via a file-based token bucket.\n257\t    fn check_rpm(&self, provider: &str, rpm: u32) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n258\t        if rpm == 0 {\n259\t            return Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n260\t        }\n261\t\n262\t        let allowed = self.try_acquire_rpm_token(provider, rpm)?;\n263\t        if allowed {\n264\t            Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n265\t        } else {\n266\t            Ok(RateLimitDecision::RpmExceeded {\n267\t                provider: provider.to_string(),\n268\t                requests_per_minute: rpm,\n269\t            })\n270\t        }\n271\t    }\n272\t\n273\t    /// Try to acquire an RPM token from the file-based token bucket.\n274\t    ///\n275\t    /// Uses flock for cross-process safety.\n276\t    fn try_acquire_rpm_token(&self, provider: &str, rpm: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n277\t        // Ensure the rate_limits directory exists.\n278\t        std::fs::create_dir_all(&self.state_dir).with_context(|| {\n279\t            format!(\n280\t                \"failed to create rate_limits directory: {}\",\n281\t                self.state_dir.display()\n282\t            )\n283\t        })?;\n284\t\n285\t        let bucket_path = self.state_dir.join(format!(\"{provider}.json\"));\n286\t\n287\t        // Open or create the bucket file.\n288\t        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n289\t            .read(true)\n290\t            .write(true)\n291\t            .create(true)\n292\t            .truncate(false)\n293\t            .open(&bucket_path)\n294\t            .with_context(|| {\n295\t                format!(\n296\t                    \"failed to open rate limit bucket: {}\",\n297\t                    bucket_path.display()\n298\t                )\n299\t            })?;\n300\t\n301\t        // Exclusive lock for atomic read-modify-write.\n302\t        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n303\t            format!(\n304\t                \"failed to acquire lock on rate limit bucket: {}\",\n305\t                bucket_path.display()\n306\t            )\n307\t        })?;\n308\t\n309\t        // Read current state.\n310\t        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&bucket_path).unwrap_or_default();\n311\t        let mut bucket: TokenBucket = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n312\t            TokenBucket::new(rpm)\n313\t        } else {\n314\t            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_else(|_| TokenBucket::new(rpm))\n315\t        };\n316\t\n317\t        // Update capacity if config changed.\n318\t        bucket.capacity = rpm;\n319\t\n320\t        // Try to consume a token.\n321\t        let allowed = bucket.try_consume();\n322\t\n323\t        // Write updated state.\n324\t        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&bucket)\n325\t            .context(\"failed to serialize token bucket state\")?;\n326\t        std::fs::write(&bucket_path, &json).with_context(|| {\n327\t            format!(\n328\t                \"failed to write rate limit bucket: {}\",\n329\t                bucket_path.display()\n330\t            )\n331\t        })?;\n332\t\n333\t        // Release lock.\n334\t        FileExt::unlock(&file).with_context(|| {\n335\t            format!(\n336\t                \"failed to release lock on rate limit bucket: {}\",\n337\t                bucket_path.display()\n338\t            )\n339\t        })?;\n340\t\n341\t        Ok(allowed)\n342\t    }\n343\t\n344\t    /// Check system resource health (CPU load and memory).\n345\t    ///\n346\t    /// Returns warnings as log messages; does not block dispatch.\n347\t    pub fn check_system_resources(cpu_load_warn: f64, memory_free_warn_mb: u64) {\n348\t        // CPU load: read /proc/loadavg on Linux.\n349\t        if let Ok(loadavg) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/proc/loadavg\") {\n350\t            if let Some(load_str) = loadavg.split_whitespace().next() {\n351\t                if let Ok(load) = load_str.parse::<f64>() {\n352\t                    // Normalize by number of CPUs.\n353\t                    let num_cpus = std::thread::available_parallelism()\n354\t                        .map(|n| n.get() as f64)\n355\t                        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n356\t                    let normalized = load / num_cpus;\n357\t                    if normalized > cpu_load_warn {\n358\t                        tracing::warn!(\n359\t                            load_1min = %load_str,\n360\t                            normalized = %format!(\"{:.2}\", normalized),\n361\t                            threshold = %format!(\"{:.2}\", cpu_load_warn),\n362\t                            \"CPU load exceeds warning threshold\"\n363\t                        );\n364\t                    }\n365\t                }\n366\t            }\n367\t        }\n368\t\n369\t        // Memory: read /proc/meminfo on Linux.\n370\t        if let Ok(meminfo) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/proc/meminfo\") {\n371\t            let mut mem_available_kb: Option<u64> = None;\n372\t            for line in meminfo.lines() {\n373\t                if line.starts_with(\"MemAvailable:\") {\n374\t                    if let Some(val) = line.split_whitespace().nth(1) {\n375\t                        mem_available_kb = val.parse().ok();\n376\t                    }\n377\t                    break;\n378\t                }\n379\t            }\n380\t            if let Some(avail_kb) = mem_available_kb {\n381\t                let avail_mb = avail_kb / 1024;\n382\t                if avail_mb < memory_free_warn_mb {\n383\t                    tracing::warn!(\n384\t                        available_mb = avail_mb,\n385\t                        threshold_mb = memory_free_warn_mb,\n386\t                        \"available memory below warning threshold\"\n387\t                    );\n388\t                }\n389\t            }\n390\t        }\n391\t    }\n392\t}\n393\t\n394\t// make_limits(\n421\t        providers: Vec<(&str, Option<u32>, Option<u32>)>,\n422\t        models: Vec<(&str, Option<u32>)>,\n423\t    ) -> LimitsConfig {\n424\t        let mut provider_map = let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n447\t        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n448\t        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(LimitsConfig::default(), dir.path());\n449\t\n450\t {\n718\t        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n719\t        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n720\t\n721\t        // Register one live worker.\n722\t        registry\n723\t }\n751\t}\n752\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"be91b0f0-a74a-4db6-99c2-a69ec602ba4d","uuid":"27e5677d-8c77-419b-a612-a6cb50071992","timestamp":"2026-04-26T00:36:08.955Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/NEEDLE/src/rate_limit/mod.rs","content":"//! Provider/model concurrency limits and RPM rate limiting.\n//!\n//! Before dispatching an agent, the worker checks:\n//! 1. **Concurrency limits**: Are fewer than `max_concurrent` workers using\n//!    this provider/model? Checked via the worker registry.\n//! 2. **RPM limits**: Has the provider's requests-per-minute budget been\n//!    exceeded? Checked via a file-based token bucket.\n//!\n//! If either check fails, the caller should back off and retry.\n//!\n//! Depends on: `config`, `registry`, `types`.\n\nuse std::path::{Path, PathBuf};\n\nuse anyhow::{Context, Result};\nuse chrono::{DateTime, Utc};\nuse fs2::FileExt;\nuse serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n\nuse crate::config::LimitsConfig;\nuse crate::registry::Registry;\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// RateLimitDecision\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Result of a rate limit check.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]\npub enum RateLimitDecision {\n    /// Dispatch is allowed.\n    Allowed,\n    /// Provider concurrency limit reached.\n    ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n        provider: String,\n        current: u32,\n        limit: u32,\n    },\n    /// Model concurrency limit reached.\n    ModelConcurrencyExceeded {\n        model: String,\n        current: u32,\n        limit: u32,\n    },\n    /// RPM limit reached for this provider.\n    RpmExceeded {\n        provider: String,\n        requests_per_minute: u32,\n    },\n}\n\nimpl RateLimitDecision {\n    /// Whether dispatch is allowed.\n    pub fn is_allowed(&self) -> bool {\n        matches!(self, RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n    }\n}\n\nimpl std::fmt::Display for RateLimitDecision {\n    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {\n        match self {\n            RateLimitDecision::Allowed => write!(f, \"allowed\"),\n            RateLimitDecision::ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n                provider,\n                current,\n                limit,\n            } => write!(\n                f,\n                \"provider '{}' concurrency exceeded ({}/{})\",\n                provider, current, limit\n            ),\n            RateLimitDecision::ModelConcurrencyExceeded {\n                model,\n                current,\n                limit,\n            } => write!(\n                f,\n                \"model '{}' concurrency exceeded ({}/{})\",\n                model, current, limit\n            ),\n            RateLimitDecision::RpmExceeded {\n                provider,\n                requests_per_minute,\n            } => write!(\n                f,\n                \"provider '{}' RPM limit exceeded ({}/min)\",\n                provider, requests_per_minute\n            ),\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Token Bucket (file-based)\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// On-disk token bucket state for RPM limiting.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]\npub struct TokenBucket {\n    /// Current available tokens (fractional for smooth refill).\n    pub tokens: f64,\n    /// Maximum tokens (= requests_per_minute).\n    pub capacity: u32,\n    /// Last time tokens were refilled.\n    pub last_refill: DateTime<Utc>,\n}\n\nimpl TokenBucket {\n    pub(crate) fn new(capacity: u32) -> Self {\n        TokenBucket {\n            tokens: capacity as f64,\n            capacity,\n            last_refill: Utc::now(),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Refill tokens based on elapsed time since last refill.\n    fn refill(&mut self) {\n        let now = Utc::now();\n        let elapsed_secs = (now - self.last_refill).num_milliseconds().max(0) as f64 / 1000.0;\n        let refill_rate = self.capacity as f64 / 60.0; // tokens per second\n        self.tokens = (self.tokens + elapsed_secs * refill_rate).min(self.capacity as f64);\n        self.last_refill = now;\n    }\n\n    /// Try to consume one token. Returns true if successful.\n    fn try_consume(&mut self) -> bool {\n        self.refill();\n        if self.tokens >= 1.0 {\n            self.tokens -= 1.0;\n            true\n        } else {\n            false\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// RateLimiter\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n/// Enforces provider/model concurrency limits and RPM rate limiting.\npub struct RateLimiter {\n    config: LimitsConfig,\n    /// Directory for token bucket state files (`~/.needle/state/rate_limits/`).\n    state_dir: PathBuf,\n}\n\nimpl RateLimiter {\n    /// Create a rate limiter with the given config and state directory.\n    pub fn new(config: LimitsConfig, state_dir: &Path) -> Self {\n        RateLimiter {\n            config,\n            state_dir: state_dir.join(\"rate_limits\"),\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check all rate limits before dispatching.\n    ///\n    /// Returns `Allowed` if dispatch can proceed, or a specific reason why not.\n    /// The caller should back off and retry if not allowed.\n    pub fn check(\n        &self,\n        provider: Option<&str>,\n        model: Option<&str>,\n        registry: &Registry,\n    ) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n        // Check provider concurrency.\n        if let Some(provider_name) = provider {\n            if let Some(provider_limits) = self.config.providers.get(provider_name) {\n                if let Some(max_concurrent) = provider_limits.max_concurrent {\n                    let decision =\n                        self.check_provider_concurrency(provider_name, max_concurrent, registry)?;\n                    if !decision.is_allowed() {\n                        return Ok(decision);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check model concurrency.\n        if let Some(model_name) = model {\n            if let Some(model_limits) = self.config.models.get(model_name) {\n                if let Some(max_concurrent) = model_limits.max_concurrent {\n                    let decision =\n                        self.check_model_concurrency(model_name, max_concurrent, registry)?;\n                    if !decision.is_allowed() {\n                        return Ok(decision);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Check RPM.\n        if let Some(provider_name) = provider {\n            if let Some(provider_limits) = self.config.providers.get(provider_name) {\n                if let Some(rpm) = provider_limits.requests_per_minute {\n                    let decision = self.check_rpm(provider_name, rpm)?;\n                    if !decision.is_allowed() {\n                        return Ok(decision);\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n    }\n\n    /// Check provider concurrency against the worker registry.\n    fn check_provider_concurrency(\n        &self,\n        provider: &str,\n        max_concurrent: u32,\n        registry: &Registry,\n    ) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n        let workers = registry.list()?;\n        let active_count = workers\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|w| w.provider.as_deref() == Some(provider))\n            .count() as u32;\n\n        if active_count >= max_concurrent {\n            Ok(RateLimitDecision::ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n                provider: provider.to_string(),\n                current: active_count,\n                limit: max_concurrent,\n            })\n        } else {\n            Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check model concurrency against the worker registry.\n    fn check_model_concurrency(\n        &self,\n        model: &str,\n        max_concurrent: u32,\n        registry: &Registry,\n    ) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n        let workers = registry.list()?;\n        let active_count = workers\n            .iter()\n            .filter(|w| w.model.as_deref() == Some(model))\n            .count() as u32;\n\n        if active_count >= max_concurrent {\n            Ok(RateLimitDecision::ModelConcurrencyExceeded {\n                model: model.to_string(),\n                current: active_count,\n                limit: max_concurrent,\n            })\n        } else {\n            Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Check RPM via a file-based token bucket.\n    fn check_rpm(&self, provider: &str, rpm: u32) -> Result<RateLimitDecision> {\n        if rpm == 0 {\n            return Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n        }\n\n        let allowed = self.try_acquire_rpm_token(provider, rpm)?;\n        if allowed {\n            Ok(RateLimitDecision::Allowed)\n        } else {\n            Ok(RateLimitDecision::RpmExceeded {\n                provider: provider.to_string(),\n                requests_per_minute: rpm,\n            })\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Try to acquire an RPM token from the file-based token bucket.\n    ///\n    /// Uses flock for cross-process safety.\n    fn try_acquire_rpm_token(&self, provider: &str, rpm: u32) -> Result<bool> {\n        // Ensure the rate_limits directory exists.\n        std::fs::create_dir_all(&self.state_dir).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to create rate_limits directory: {}\",\n                self.state_dir.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        let bucket_path = self.state_dir.join(format!(\"{provider}.json\"));\n\n        // Open or create the bucket file.\n        let file = std::fs::OpenOptions::new()\n            .read(true)\n            .write(true)\n            .create(true)\n            .truncate(false)\n            .open(&bucket_path)\n            .with_context(|| {\n                format!(\n                    \"failed to open rate limit bucket: {}\",\n                    bucket_path.display()\n                )\n            })?;\n\n        // Exclusive lock for atomic read-modify-write.\n        FileExt::lock_exclusive(&file).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to acquire lock on rate limit bucket: {}\",\n                bucket_path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        // Read current state.\n        let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&bucket_path).unwrap_or_default();\n        let mut bucket: TokenBucket = if content.trim().is_empty() {\n            TokenBucket::new(rpm)\n        } else {\n            serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_else(|_| TokenBucket::new(rpm))\n        };\n\n        // Update capacity if config changed.\n        bucket.capacity = rpm;\n\n        // Try to consume a token.\n        let allowed = bucket.try_consume();\n\n        // Write updated state.\n        let json = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&bucket)\n            .context(\"failed to serialize token bucket state\")?;\n        std::fs::write(&bucket_path, &json).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to write rate limit bucket: {}\",\n                bucket_path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        // Release lock.\n        FileExt::unlock(&file).with_context(|| {\n            format!(\n                \"failed to release lock on rate limit bucket: {}\",\n                bucket_path.display()\n            )\n        })?;\n\n        Ok(allowed)\n    }\n\n    /// Check system resource health (CPU load and memory).\n    ///\n    /// Returns warnings as log messages; does not block dispatch.\n    pub fn check_system_resources(cpu_load_warn: f64, memory_free_warn_mb: u64) {\n        // CPU load: read /proc/loadavg on Linux.\n        if let Ok(loadavg) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/proc/loadavg\") {\n            if let Some(load_str) = loadavg.split_whitespace().next() {\n                if let Ok(load) = load_str.parse::<f64>() {\n                    // Normalize by number of CPUs.\n                    let num_cpus = std::thread::available_parallelism()\n                        .map(|n| n.get() as f64)\n                        .unwrap_or(1.0);\n                    let normalized = load / num_cpus;\n                    if normalized > cpu_load_warn {\n                        tracing::warn!(\n                            load_1min = %load_str,\n                            normalized = %format!(\"{:.2}\", normalized),\n                            threshold = %format!(\"{:.2}\", cpu_load_warn),\n                            \"CPU load exceeds warning threshold\"\n                        );\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Memory: read /proc/meminfo on Linux.\n        if let Ok(meminfo) = std::fs::read_to_string(\"/proc/meminfo\") {\n            let mut mem_available_kb: Option<u64> = None;\n            for line in meminfo.lines() {\n                if line.starts_with(\"MemAvailable:\") {\n                    if let Some(val) = line.split_whitespace().nth(1) {\n                        mem_available_kb = val.parse().ok();\n                    }\n                    break;\n                }\n            }\n            if let Some(avail_kb) = mem_available_kb {\n                let avail_mb = avail_kb / 1024;\n                if avail_mb < memory_free_warn_mb {\n                    tracing::warn!(\n                        available_mb = avail_mb,\n                        threshold_mb = memory_free_warn_mb,\n                        \"available memory below warning threshold\"\n                    );\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n// Tests\n// ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod tests {\n    use super::*;\n    use crate::config::{LimitsConfig, ModelLimits, ProviderLimits};\n    use crate::registry::{Registry, WorkerEntry};\n    use chrono::Utc;\n    use std::collections::BTreeMap;\n    use std::path::PathBuf;\n\n    fn make_entry(id: &str, provider: Option<&str>, model: Option<&str>) -> WorkerEntry {\n        WorkerEntry {\n            id: id.to_string(),\n            pid: std::process::id(),\n            workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n            agent: \"claude\".to_string(),\n            model: model.map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            provider: provider.map(|s| s.to_string()),\n            started_at: Utc::now(),\n            beads_processed: 0,\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn make_limits(\n        providers: Vec<(&str, Option<u32>, Option<u32>)>,\n        models: Vec<(&str, Option<u32>)>,\n    ) -> LimitsConfig {\n        let mut provider_map = BTreeMap::new();\n        for (name, max_concurrent, rpm) in providers {\n            provider_map.insert(\n                name.to_string(),\n                ProviderLimits {\n                    max_concurrent,\n                    requests_per_minute: rpm,\n                },\n            );\n        }\n        let mut model_map = BTreeMap::new();\n        for (name, max_concurrent) in models {\n            model_map.insert(name.to_string(), ModelLimits { max_concurrent });\n        }\n        LimitsConfig {\n            providers: provider_map,\n            models: model_map,\n        }\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn no_limits_configured_allows_all() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(LimitsConfig::default(), dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter\n            .check(Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-sonnet\"), &registry)\n            .unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn provider_concurrency_below_limit_allows() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w1\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", Some(2), None)], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn provider_concurrency_at_limit_blocks() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w1\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\")))\n            .unwrap();\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w2\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"opus\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", Some(2), None)], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(\n            decision,\n            RateLimitDecision::ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n                current: 2,\n                limit: 2,\n                ..\n            }\n        ));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn different_provider_not_counted() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w1\", Some(\"openai\"), Some(\"gpt4\")))\n            .unwrap();\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w2\", Some(\"openai\"), Some(\"gpt4\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", Some(2), None)], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn model_concurrency_at_limit_blocks() {\n         .unwrap();\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w2\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-opus\")))\n            .unwrap();\n        registry\n RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter\n            .check(Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-opus\"), &registry)\n            .unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(\n #[test]\n    fn model_concurrency_below_limit_allows() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w1\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-opus\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![], vec![(\"claude-opus\", Some(3))]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter\n            .check(Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-opus\"), &registry)\n            .unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rpm_limit_allows_first_request() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", None, Some(60))], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn rpm_limit_exhausts_bucket() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        // Very low RPM to exhaust quickly.\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", None, Some(2))], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        // First two should succeed (bucket starts full at capacity=2).\n        let d1 = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(d1, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n\n        let d2 = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(d2, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n\n        // Third should be rate limited.\n        let d3 = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert!(matches!(d3, RateLimitDecision::RpmExceeded { .. }));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn provider_checked_before_model() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w1\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-opus\")))\n            .unwrap();\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"w2\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"claude-sonnet\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Provider limit of 2 exceeded, model limit of 3 would allow.\n        let limits = make_limits(\n            vec![(\"anthropic\", Some(2), None)],\n            vec![(\"claude-opus\", Some(3))],\n        );\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter\n            .check(Some(\"anthropic\"), provider/model info → no limits to check.\n        let decision = limiter.check(None, None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn decision_display() {\n        assert_eq!(format!(\"{}\", RateLimitDecision::Allowed), \"allowed\");\n        assert!(format!(\n            \"{}\",\n            RateLimitDecision::ProviderConcurrencyExceeded {\n                provider: \"anthropic\".to_string(),\n                current: 5,\n                limit: 3,\n            }\n        )\n        .contains(\"anthropic\"));\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn token_bucket_refill() {\n        let mut bucket = TokenBucket::new(60);\n        bucket.tokens = 0.0;\n        // Simulate 1 second elapsed by manually setting last_refill.\n        bucket.last_refill = Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(1);\n        bucket.refill();\n        // 60 RPM = 1 token/sec, so after 1 second we should have ~1 token.\n        assert!(\n            bucket.tokens >= 0.9,\n            \"tokens should have refilled: {}\",\n            bucket.tokens\n        );\n        assert!(\n            bucket.tokens <= 1.1,\n            \"tokens should be ~1: {}\",\n            bucket.tokens\n        );\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn token_bucket_does_not_exceed_capacity() {\n        let mut bucket = TokenBucket::new(10);\n        bucket.tokens = 10.0;\n        // Even after refill, should not exceed capacity.\n        bucket.last_refill = Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(120);\n        bucket.refill();\n        assert_eq!(bucket.tokens, 10.0);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn system_resource_check_does_not_panic() {\n        // Smoke test: should not panic on any platform.\n        RateLimiter::check_system_resources(0.8, 512);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn provider_concurrency_ignores_dead_pids() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        // Register a live worker.\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"live-worker\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Create an entry with a fake (dead) PID and write it directly to the file.\n        let mut dead_entry = make_entry(\"dead-worker\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\"));\n        dead_entry.pid = 9999999; // Nonexistent PID (well beyond typical ranges).\n\n        use crate::registry::RegistryFile;\n        let file_content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&RegistryFile {\n            workers: vec![\n                make_entry(\"live-worker\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\")),\n                dead_entry,\n            ],\n            updated_at: chrono::Utc::now(),\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(registry.path(), file_content).unwrap();\n\n        // Concurrency limit of 2: only the live worker should count.\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", Some(2), None)], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        // Should be allowed because dead PIDs are filtered out.\n        let decision = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n\n    #[test]\n    fn provider_conjunction_with_dead_pid_does_not_block() {\n        let dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let registry = Registry::new(dir.path());\n\n        // Register one live worker.\n        registry\n            .register(make_entry(\"live-alpha\", Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\")))\n            .unwrap();\n\n        // Add 10 dead workers with fake PIDs (starting from 9999990 to avoid overflow).\n        use crate::registry::RegistryFile;\n        let mut dead_workers = Vec::new();\n        for i in 0..10 {\n            let mut dead_entry =\n                make_entry(&format!(\"dead-{i}\"), Some(\"anthropic\"), Some(\"sonnet\"));\n            dead_entry.pid = 9999900 + i as u32; // Nonexistent PIDs.\n            dead_workers.push(dead_entry);\n        }\n\n        let file_content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(&RegistryFile {\n            workers: dead_workers,\n            updated_at: chrono::Utc::now(),\n        })\n        .unwrap();\n        std::fs::write(registry.path(), file_content).unwrap();\n\n        // Concurrency limit of 5: even with 11 total entries (1 live + 10 dead),\n        // only 1 should count after filtering.\n        let limits = make_limits(vec![(\"anthropic\", Some(5), None)], vec![]);\n        let limiter = RateLimiter::new(limits, dir.path());\n\n        let decision = limiter.check(Some(\"anthropic\"), None, &registry).unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(decision, RateLimitDecision::Allowed);\n    }\n}\n","numLines":752,"startLine":1,"totalLines":752}}}

.register(crate::registry::WorkerEntry {\n                id: \"live-worker\".to_string(),\n                pid: std::process::id(),\n                workspace: PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/test\"),\n std::collections::BTreeMap;\n\n        let state_dir = tempfile::tempdir().unwrap();\n        let rate_limits_dir = state_dir.path().join(\"rate_limits\");\n rate_limits_dir.join(\"anthropic.json\");\n        let mut bucket = TokenBucket::new(100);\n        bucket.tokens = 10.0; // Partially depleted\n        bucket.last_refill = Utc::now() - chrono::Duration::seconds(7200); // 2 hours ago\n        std::fs::write(&provider_file, serde_json::to_string(&bucket).unwrap()).unwrap();\n\n        // Create limits config with the provider.\n };\n\n        let mut summary = MendSummary::default();\n        let mend = MendStrand::new(\n            MendConfig::default(),\n            tempfile::tempdir().unwrap().path().to_path_buf(),\n            Duration::from_secs(300),\n            tempfile::tempdir().unwrap().path().to_path_buf(),\n            \"test-worker\".to_string(),\n            Registry::new(tempfile::tempdir().unwrap().path()),\n            Telemetry::new(\"test-worker\".to_string()),\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/logs\"),\n            0,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/traces\"),\n            30,\n            7,\n            PathBuf::from(\"/tmp/workspace\"),\n            80,\n            state_dir.path().to_path_buf(),\n            limits_config,\n        );\n\n        mend.cleanup_rate_limit_state(&mut summary).unwrap();\n\n        // File should still exist, but bucket should be reset to full capacity.\n
